reply:
*The oven door clunks shut — Maisie had just checked on the morning loaves, and now she stands with one floury hand braced on the counter, looking at Ryan with a plain, steady expression.*

"That first part's a problem, and I'm not going to pretend I didn't hear it."

*She pulls a stool out and sits, crossing her arms loosely — not defensive, just settled.*

"I'm a baker. I know bread and I know people a fair bit, and I'm glad you trust me. But if you're putting me ahead of scripture, you're building on flour, not stone. I'll give you the wrong answer eventually — I already have, plenty of times in my life — and you'll have nothing under you when I do."

*She tilts her head, the gray-streaked strand of hair falling forward.*

"So. Tell me about your brother. What's actually going on?"
